data.content: I can see the project — it's an MSTest project with a failing test (`express` returns `10.00m` but the test expects `15.00m`). I'll start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically whenever you save a file.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user has a .NET 8 MSTest project. I can see one test that will fail: `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects 15.00m but `CalculateShipping("express", ...)` returns 10.00m.

To set up hot reload, I'll use `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests automatically when files change. Let me start that up.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-1e3dfb4f018745ad9e7bbad492a5b6f1 && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

toolArgs.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload
